protocol.name: cosmic_corner_radius_v1
protocol.copyright:
Copyright © 2024 System76

protocol.description:
This protocol provides a way for clients to communicate the corner radius of their toplevels, should they use rounded corners.
This hint can then be used by the compositor to draw fitting outlines or prevent overdrawing of other server-side drawn interfaces.

interface.name: cosmic_corner_radius_manager_v1
interface.description: Manager for creating corner-radius objects for existing surfaces

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the server, that the client will no longer use this global. Any previously created corner-radius objects remain valid until destroyed.

request.name: get_corner_radius

description.summary: Create a new corner-radius object for an existing toplevel

request.description:
Instantiate an interface extension for the given xdg_toplevel to specify their corner radius.
If the given xdg_toplevel already has a cosmic_corner_radius_toplevel_v1 object associated, the corner_radius_exists protocol error will be raised.
Deprecated: Use `get_corner_radius_surface` instead.

request.name: get_corner_radius_surface

description.summary: Create a new corner-radius object for an existing xdg-surface

request.description:
Instantiate an interface extension for the given xdg_surface to specify their corner radius.
If the given xdg_surface already has a cosmic_corner_radius_toplevel_v1 object associated, the corner_radius_exists protocol error will be raised.
It is an error submitting this request for a xdg+surface object, that doesn't have a role associated yet. (See `xdg_surface::get_toplevel` or `xdg_surface::get_popup`.) A no_role protocol error will be raised in this case.

request.name: get_corner_radius_layer

description.summary: Create a new corner-radius object for an existing layer

request.description:
Instantiate an interface extension for the given layer-surface to specify their corner radius.
If the given layer-suface already has a cosmic_corner_radius_layer_v1 object associated, the corner_radius_exists protocol error will be raised.

entry.name: corner_radius_exists
entry.summary: the surface already has a corner-radius object

entry.name: no_role
entry.summary: the surface already has a corner-radius object

interface.name: cosmic_corner_radius_toplevel_v1
interface.description:
The corner-radius object provides a way to specify a corner-radius for it's associated toplevel.
If the xdg_toplevel associated with the cosmic_corner_radius_toplevel_v1 object has been destroyed, this object becomes inert. Any further requests other than destroy will raise the toplevel_destroyed protocol error.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the server that the client will no longer be using this protocol object. The corner radius will be unset on the next commit.

request.name: set_radius
request.description:
This request sets the hinted corner radius values for rectangular windows.
The corner radius hint is double-buffered state and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.
The value is given in logical space relative to the window geometry of the associated xdg_toplevel. If any value exceeds a quarter of either dimension of the window geometry the radius_too_large protocol error is raised.

request.name: unset_radius
request.description: Unsets any previously hinted corner radius values without invalidating the object for later use. Can be used by clients that possibly have temporary irregular shapes.

entry.name: toplevel_destroyed
entry.summary: the associated toplevel object has been already destroyed

entry.name: radius_too_large
entry.summary: the associated toplevel's window geometry isn't large enough for the requested radius

interface.name: cosmic_corner_radius_layer_v1
interface.description:
The corner-radius object provides a way to specify a corner-radius for it's associated layer surface.
If the zwlr_layer_surface_v1 associated with the cosmic_corner_radius_layer_v1 object has been destroyed, this object becomes inert. Any further requests other than destroy will raise the layer_destroyed protocol error.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the server that the client will no longer be using this protocol object. The corner radius will be unset on the next commit.

request.name: set_radius
request.description:
This request sets the hinted corner radius values for rectangular layers.
The corner radius hint is double-buffered state and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.
The value is given in logical space relative to the remaining size of the associated zwlr_layer_surface_v1 after any said padding was applied. If any value exceeds half of the respective dimension of the remaining size the radius_too_large protocol error is raised.

request.name: unset_radius
request.description: Unsets any previously hinted corner radius values without invalidating the object for later use. Can be used by clients that possibly have temporary irregular shapes.

request.name: set_padding
request.description:
This request sets the hinted padding values for rectangular layers.
The padding hint is double-buffered state and will be applied on the next wl_surface.commit.
The value is given in logical space as the amount of pixels to subtract from the size of the associated zwlr_layer_surface and in the case of the top and left padding added to the layer position before applying the corner radius. Negative padding values are allowed.
If any value exceeds half of the respective dimension of the surface size the padding_too_large protocol error is raised.

request.name: unset_padding
request.description: Unsets any previously hinted padding values without invalidating the object for later use. Can be used by clients that possibly have temporary irregular shapes.

entry.name: layer_destroyed
entry.summary: the associated layer surface object has been already destroyed

entry.name: radius_too_large
entry.summary: the associated layer's size isn't large enough for the requested radius

entry.name: padding_too_large
entry.summary: the associated layer's size isn't large enough for the requested padding
